agent_description: DeepSentinel is a security vulnerability scanner that analyzes source code for common security weaknesses. It uses an LLM to identify vulnerabilities across 10 CWE categories including hardcoded credentials, SQL injection, command injection, XSS, and others. The agent returns structured findings with severity levels and provides recommendations for code approval, review, or blocking based on risk assessment.

code.description: The source code to analyze for security vulnerabilities

file_path.description: Path or name of the file being analyzed (optional, defaults to 'unknown')

context.description: Additional context about the code or analysis requirements (optional)

findings.description: List of security vulnerabilities found in the code, each with severity, CWE ID, title, line number, description, and fix recommendation

risk_level.description: Overall risk level based on the highest severity finding

recommendation.description: Action recommendation based on risk level

findings_count.description: Total number of security findings identified

raw_analysis.description: Raw LLM response when JSON parsing fails (fallback only)

consistency_rules.description: CRITICAL/HIGH risk should map to BLOCK, MEDIUM to REVIEW, LOW/NONE to APPROVE
Zero findings should result in NONE risk level, non-zero findings should not be NONE

policy.purpose: Analyzes source code for security vulnerabilities across 10 CWE categories and provides risk-based recommendations for code approval workflows.
domain_rules: Must scan for exactly 10 CWE categories: 798, 89, 78, 79, 22, 327, 502, 918, 601, 95
Hardcoded credentials include passwords, API keys, and secrets in source code (inferred)
Weak cryptography includes MD5/SHA1 for passwords and weak ciphers (inferred)
Path traversal requires unsanitized input in file paths (inferred)
Risk level must match the highest individual finding severity (inferred)

domain_edge_cases.correct_handling: Return empty findings array with NONE risk level and APPROVE recommendation (inferred)
Return UNKNOWN risk level with REVIEW recommendation and include raw analysis (inferred)

terminology.Finding: Individual vulnerability with severity, CWE ID, description, and fix

output_constraints: Must return structured JSON with findings array, risk_level, recommendation, and findings_count
Each finding requires: severity, cwe_id, title, line_number, description, fix
Findings count must equal length of findings array
tool_requirements: System prompt must specify all 10 CWE categories and JSON schema requirements
Must request pure JSON response without markdown formatting
quality_expectations: Enforce consistency between risk level and recommendation through post-processing
Attempt multiple JSON extraction methods before fallback
Provide actionable fix recommendations for each finding
